title: TYPING not rate-limited allows one authed client to flood all N peers at wire speed
description: ws.rs TYPING (~line 1918) is explicitly exempt from check_rate_limit per DESIGN.md. But TYPING calls state.broadcast() to ALL connected clients. A single authenticated client (no subscription needed) can send TYPING at full network speed, forcing O(N) mpsc channel sends per frame across all N connected client write tasks. At 1000 clients this saturates all write queues with typing notifications. The DESIGN.md exemption was motivated by UX latency, not an analysis of amplification cost. Fix: add a separate per-sender TYPING rate cap (e.g. 2/sec) independent of the broadcast counter, so normal usage is unaffected but abuse is blocked.
